note: THIS IS NOT A WRITE TO THE PRODUCTION HYPOTHESIS REGISTRY (lottery_api/data/hypothesis_registry.jsonl). It is a frozen design snapshot under outputs/research/, mirroring the hypothesis_registry_draft from p268c_draw_order_full_history_feasibility_and_hypothesis_design_20260610.json, produced as P268D step 1 (freeze_registry_artifact) ahead of the bounded full-history backfill (P268D step 2, this same task). A future, separately authorized task is required to perform the actual production Hypothesis Registry write. NOT WRITTEN TO production registry; draft only.
p267c_conclusion_boundary: P267C (M3+ revalidation) found NO_VALIDATED_M3_EDGE across 36/36 cells for the existing replay-backed strategy family. This does not close the broader success-rate / hit-rate research line.
p268b_conclusion_boundary: P268B (PR #407, merged into main @ f3c933b) is a diagnostics-only prototype. drawNumberAppear field structurally valid for all 154 sampled main-pool records (BIG_LOTTO/POWER_LOTTO/DAILY_539) and 52/52 each (3_STAR/4_STAR), sorted-equivalent to drawNumberSize. DB alignment = NO_LOCAL_ROWS. No hit-rate or success-rate claim made.
track_b_isolation: winnerCount / prize-distribution / EV (Track B, P268A Direction B) is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this hit-rate design line and must not be merged into H1/H2/H3 or any P268D step.
primary_game_rationale: Largest available draw depth (~5,880 draws), maximizing statistical power for the within-draw permutation test; BIG_LOTTO and POWER_LOTTO are treated as secondary/co-primary at governance discretion.

hypotheses.name: per_ball_exit_rank_heterogeneity
hypotheses.statement: Per-ball mean exit-rank (position within drawNumberAppear, conditional on the ball being drawn) is heterogeneous across balls, beyond what a within-draw fully-exchangeable permutation null predicts.
hypotheses.null_model: Within-draw permutation null: condition on the observed drawn set per draw; all orderings of that set are equally likely. Generate >=10,000 permutation replicates per game.
hypotheses.test_statistic: Dispersion (e.g., variance or chi2-style statistic) of standardized per-ball mean exit-rank across all balls in the pool.
hypotheses.window: Estimation window only (see windows.estimation_window below); 2026-04 and 2026-05 EXCLUDED from any confirmatory test (already inspected in P268B).
hypotheses.correction: Bonferroni across games tested (denominator = number of games included in this round; if DAILY_539-only design is chosen, no cross-game correction needed for H1 itself).
hypotheses.pass_criteria: Estimation-window corrected permutation p < 0.01 AND the same direction/effect replicates with holdout-window p < 0.05 (see H1_holdout below).
hypotheses.fail_criteria: Estimation-window corrected p >= 0.01, OR holdout replication fails -> classify as NULL / diagnostics-only, permanent closure artifact, H2 NOT opened.

hypotheses.name: exit_rank_heterogeneity_holdout_replication
hypotheses.statement: The per-ball exit-rank heterogeneity pattern identified in the H1 estimation window replicates (same direction, p<0.05) in the disjoint holdout window.
hypotheses.gating: Only computed if H1 estimation-window result passes its threshold. Holdout window is sealed/unopened until then.

hypotheses.name: earliness_score_predicts_inclusion_frequency
hypotheses.statement: A per-ball 'earliness score' derived strictly from the estimation window predicts the direction of inclusion-frequency deviation (vs uniform baseline) in the holdout window.
hypotheses.null_model: Inclusion frequency under uniform random draw (closed-form per game, e.g., C(n-1,k-1)/C(n,k) per ball per draw).
hypotheses.test_design: Single pre-registered one-tailed test of correlation (e.g., Spearman) between estimation-window earliness score and holdout-window inclusion-frequency deviation. NOT a scan over alternative score definitions.
hypotheses.correction: None required if pre-registered as a single test; any deviation from the pre-registered score definition voids the test.
hypotheses.pass_criteria: One-tailed p < 0.05 in the pre-registered direction.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: p >= 0.05 or wrong-direction -> diagnostics-only closure; this does NOT reopen H1.

hypotheses.name: earliness_score_temporal_stability
hypotheses.statement: The per-ball earliness score is stable across a split-half (or era-split, if any segmentation becomes possible) of the estimation window.
hypotheses.test_design: Spearman rank correlation of earliness scores between the two halves.
hypotheses.fail_criteria: Non-significant or negative correlation -> treat H1/H2 results as fragile / non-stable, do not proceed to strategy design even if H1/H2 nominally pass.
hypotheses.gating: Computed alongside H1_holdout; informs robustness assessment, not a hard gate on its own but a required input to any future go/no-go.

windows.full_history_range: 2007-01 to most recent available month, EXCLUDING 2026-04 and 2026-05 (already inspected by P268B prototype -- excluded from confirmatory analysis to avoid look-ahead contamination)
windows.split: 70% estimation / 30% holdout, split chronologically (estimation = earliest 70% of eligible draws, holdout = most recent 30% of eligible draws, both excluding 2026-04/05)
windows.split_half_for_h3: Estimation window further split chronologically in half for H3.

baseline.h1_baseline: Within-draw permutation null (exchangeable ordering given the drawn set).
baseline.h2_baseline: Closed-form uniform inclusion probability per ball given pool size and draw size.

p221f_p_hacking_guardrails: All hypotheses, windows, baselines, and pass/fail thresholds frozen BEFORE any full-history data is fetched (this artifact = registry-freeze, precedes the bounded backfill in this same task).
2026-04/05 permanently excluded from confirmatory tests (P268B already inspected this window).
Holdout window sealed/not computed until H1 estimation-window result is finalized.
H2 score definition fixed at registration time; any later change to the score definition requires a new hypothesis ID (no silent redefinition).
No scan over alternative null models, statistics, or windows after seeing results; any such exploration must be logged as a separate, clearly-labeled exploratory (non-confirmatory) note.
Single primary game (DAILY_539) for H1 minimizes multiple-testing burden; if co-primary games are added, Bonferroni applies across the co-primary set.
Track B (winnerCount/EV) variables must not be added to H1/H2/H3 feature sets.
this_task_scope: Step 2 (bounded_rate_full_history_fetcher): produced by analysis/p268d1_draw_order_full_history_artifact_backfill.py and its outputs/research/p268d1_*.jsonl / .ledger.json / .summary.json artifacts.

Steps 3-10 (checkpoint resume across future runs, structure validation aggregate, ZIP/CSV cross-check, DB alignment, data-quality gate, H1 test, pass/fail branch, governance closeout) are explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this task.
explicit_non_claims: No claim that hit-rate / success-rate has been improved.
No production DB write performed or authorized by this artifact.
No Hypothesis Registry write performed (this is a design-snapshot freeze, not a registry write).
No H1/H2/H3 statistical test, permutation test, or p-value computed in this task.
Track B (winnerCount/EV/popularity) is not part of this hit-rate design line.
